# CodingPlanAuthKit

> Swift package that lets iOS 17+ / macOS 14+ apps sign users into their existing AI coding-plan accounts (OpenAI Codex / ChatGPT today; designed for more providers next) via OAuth 2.0 + PKCE, persists credentials in the system Keychain, and exposes plan-bound API clients. The goal is "bring your own account": the user signs in, the app inherits their plan quota — no API keys, no per-token billing.

The repository ships **two SPM products** from one package:

- `CodingPlanAuthKit` — pure auth (OAuth + PKCE + Keychain). Depends only on [SwiftWebServer](https://github.com/atom2ueki/SwiftWebServer).
- `CodingPlanCodex` — plan-bound API clients for the ChatGPT backend (Codex chat, usage / rate limits). Depends on `CodingPlanAuthKit`.

Adopters pick either or both. The split is deliberate: a consumer who only needs OAuth shouldn't have to take the API plumbing along.

Architecture is **Hexagonal / Ports & Adapters + Strategy**. Domain protocols live in `Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/` (`AuthProvider`, `LoginSession`, `TokenStorage`, `HTTPClient`); concrete adapters live in `Infrastructure/` (HTTP, OAuth, Keychain, callback server) and `Providers/` (OpenAI today). `AuthService` is the actor-based registry that keys `AuthProvider` strategies by id and orchestrates login/refresh/logout. `OAuth2PKCEFlow` is a reusable engine that handles PKCE generation, the local callback server, the authorization URL, the auth-code exchange, and token refresh — so a new provider is essentially `OAuthConfig` + an `OAuth2TokenResponseParser` conformance.

Swift 6 strict concurrency is enabled; `ExistentialAny` and `InferIsolatedConformances` are turned on as upcoming features.

## Auth core (`CodingPlanAuthKit`)

- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/AuthProvider.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/AuthProvider.swift): `AuthProvider` and `LoginSession` protocols — every provider's contract.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/Credentials.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/Credentials.swift): `Credentials` value type holding access/refresh/id tokens plus account metadata pulled from JWT claims.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/TokenStorage.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/TokenStorage.swift): `TokenStorage` protocol — pluggable persistence.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/TokenType.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/TokenType.swift): `TokenType` extensible string-typed enum (`.bearer`).
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/AuthError.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Core/AuthError.swift): `AuthError` typed errors with `LocalizedError` conformance; `tokenExchangeFailed(statusCode:message:)` carries the HTTP status.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Application/AuthService.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Application/AuthService.swift): the actor registry. Registers providers, refreshes tokens transparently, persists via the injected `TokenStorage`.

## Presentation (SwiftUI / AppKit)

- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Presentation/AuthState.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Presentation/AuthState.swift): `@Observable @MainActor` view-model glue scoped to one provider id.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Presentation/BrowserAuthSession.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Presentation/BrowserAuthSession.swift): `ASWebAuthenticationSession` wrapper with `async`/`await` semantics and a custom-scheme callback path.

## OAuth 2.0 + PKCE engine

- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2PKCEFlow.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2PKCEFlow.swift): the reusable flow — `beginLogin()` and `refresh(credentials:)`. Switchable refresh body encoding (`.formURLEncoded` or `.json` for OpenAI).
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2LoginSession.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2LoginSession.swift): the generic `LoginSession` actor returned by the engine.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2TokenResponseParser.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2TokenResponseParser.swift): protocol providers conform to for their JWT claim shape.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuthConfig.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuthConfig.swift): provider-agnostic OAuth endpoint + scope config.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/PKCE.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/PKCE.swift): S256 PKCE generator.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2Helpers.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/OAuth/OAuth2Helpers.swift): `randomState`, `formURLEncoded`.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/Server/LocalCallbackServer.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/Server/LocalCallbackServer.swift): localhost HTTP server that intercepts the OAuth redirect.

## HTTP and storage

- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/HTTP/HTTPClient.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/HTTP/HTTPClient.swift): `HTTPClient` protocol + `URLSessionHTTPClient` + `HTTPMethod` / `HTTPRequest` / `HTTPResponse`. Buffered only — streaming lives in `CodingPlanCodex` directly on `URLSession.bytes(for:)`.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/Storage/KeychainTokenStorage.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Infrastructure/Storage/KeychainTokenStorage.swift): default `TokenStorage` adapter. Defaults service prefix to `Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier`. Optional `accessGroup:` parameter for sharing credentials across an app + extension via Keychain Access Group.

## OpenAI provider (auth)

- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAIAuthProvider.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAIAuthProvider.swift): ~50-line adapter wrapping `OAuth2PKCEFlow`.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAIOAuthConfig.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAIOAuthConfig.swift): client id, endpoints, scopes matching the official Codex CLI flow.
- [Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAITokenResponseParser.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanAuthKit/Providers/OpenAI/Auth/OpenAITokenResponseParser.swift): parses `chatgpt_account_id`, `chatgpt_plan_type`, and email out of the access / id token JWT claims.

## Plan-bound API clients (`CodingPlanCodex`)

- [Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAICodexClient.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAICodexClient.swift): `createTextResponse` (buffered) and `streamTextResponse` (live `AsyncThrowingStream<String>` of SSE deltas via `URLSession.bytes(for:)`).
- [Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAICodexUsageClient.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAICodexUsageClient.swift): `fetchRateLimits` returns the plan's primary + secondary windows and credit balance.
- [Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAIBackend.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanCodex/OpenAIBackend.swift): shared `chatgpt.com/backend-api` base URL + originator constants.
- [Sources/CodingPlanCodex/CodexError.swift](./Sources/CodingPlanCodex/CodexError.swift): structured errors: `.missingAccountId`, `.backendError(statusCode: Int?, message: String)` (status nil for SSE-event failures with HTTP 200), `.invalidResponse`.

## Tests

- [Tests/CodingPlanAuthKitTests/](./Tests/CodingPlanAuthKitTests): unit tests for the OAuth flow, PKCE, callback server (real ports), token storage. `MockHTTPClient` injects buffered HTTP responses.
- [Tests/CodingPlanCodexTests/](./Tests/CodingPlanCodexTests): tests for the Codex / usage clients against a duplicate `MockHTTPClient`.

## Build, packaging, CI

- [Package.swift](./Package.swift): `swift-tools-version: 6.1`, two products, two test targets, `ExistentialAny` and `InferIsolatedConformances` upcoming features enabled.
- [.github/workflows/swift.yml](./.github/workflows/swift.yml): `swift build` + `swift test` on macOS-15.
- [.github/dependabot.yml](./.github/dependabot.yml): weekly SwiftPM + GitHub Actions update PRs.

## Adding a new provider

The auth side of a new provider (Anthropic, Google, etc.) requires three small pieces and no changes to `Core/` or the registry:

1. A `XxxOAuthConfig` static factory returning an `OAuthConfig` (client id, endpoints, scopes).
2. A `XxxTokenResponseParser: OAuth2TokenResponseParser` that parses the provider's JWT claim shape into `Credentials`.
3. A thin `XxxAuthProvider: AuthProvider` actor that constructs an `OAuth2PKCEFlow` with those two and delegates `beginLogin()` / `refresh(credentials:)` to it.

Plan-bound API clients (the analog of `CodingPlanCodex` for that provider) are a separate concern and should ship in their own SPM target / product so adopters who only want auth don't take them along.
